\section{IMPORTANT SAFETY INFORMATION}

\safetywarning{INSTRUCTIONS PERTAINING TO RISK OF FIRE, ELECTRIC SHOCK, OR INJURY TO PERSONS}

\begin{safetytwocol}

\safetylead{Always follow these basic precautions when using this product.}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Read all the instructions before using the product.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Do not allow children to play on the product. Close supervision of children is necessary when the product is used near children.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Avoid placing hands or fingers inside the product.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Stop using the product immediately if it has been physically damaged or modified. Improper use may cause unpredictable behavior, leading to fire, explosion, or injury.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
If the product overheats, emits unusual odors or smoke, leaks, or burns, stop using it immediately and contact customer support.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Never attempt to open, repair, or modify the product. Any tampering may result in electric shock, fire, or battery damage.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Be aware that liquid ejected from the product may cause irritation or burns. Avoid contact with leaking liquid and seek medical help if it contacts your eyes.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Do not expose the product to fire or excessive temperatures. Doing so may result in an explosion if the temperature exceeds the rated limit.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Any use of unrecommended or non\sphinxhyphen{}supplied materials or parts may result in a risk of fire, electric shock, or personal injury.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Do not leave the battery charging unattended for long periods. Always monitor the charging process to ensure safe operation.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
To reduce the risk of electric shock, unplug the product from any power source before attempting any technical service or troubleshooting.

\end{itemize}

\end{safetytwocol}

\safetysubbar{OPERATING INSTRUCTIONS}

\begin{safetytwocol}

\safetylead{SAVE THESE INSTRUCTIONS}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Stop using the product immediately if it shows signs of damage. Discontinue use and contact customer support for assistance.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Do not charge the battery in extremely hot or cold environments and strictly adhere to the specified operating temperature ranges.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Charging temperature: \sphinxhyphen{}4°F to 113°F (\sphinxhyphen{}20°C to 45°C).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Discharging temperature: \sphinxhyphen{}4°F to 113°F (\sphinxhyphen{}20°C to 45°C).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
To ensure proper air circulation, keep the product vents uncovered.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Charging in damp or poorly ventilated spaces may cause safety hazards.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Water can cause short circuits or damage to the charger, leading to safety risks.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Unplug the power cord from a power outlet during a storm.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Immediately turn off the product if it has fallen, been dropped, or exposed to vibrations.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Ensure the device(s) are powered off before connecting them to the product.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Do not charge the product using a damaged or broken charging cord or plug.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Do not use the product to charge any device with a damaged or broken cable or plug.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Always unplug the charging cord by pulling the plug, not the cord, to reduce the risk of damage.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Ensure the product is properly secured when transporting it in a moving vehicle.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
DO NOT place the unit upside down or on its side during use or storage.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
DO NOT place the product on the floor or at a height less than 18 inches (457 mm) above the floor during operation in a workshop or repair facility.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
DO NOT use the product’s accessories with other devices or equipment.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Solar charge time depends on weather conditions. Place your solar panel where it will get as much direct sunlight as possible.

\end{itemize}

\end{safetytwocol}

\HBApplyLang{en}

\section{Meaning of Symbols}
\label{\detokenize{index:meaning-of-symbols}}

\subsection{User Maintenance Instructions}
\label{\detokenize{index:user-maintenance-instructions}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
During the lifecycle of energy storage products, a certain degree of capacity and energy degradation is expected.
As the number of charge and discharge cycles increases and storage time extends, this degradation will gradually intensify,
which is a normal phenomenon consistent with the natural aging of battery cells.


\subsection{Signal Words}
\label{\detokenize{index:signal-words}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{CAUTION}: Hazardous practices that may result in personal injury and/or property damage.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{WARNING}: Hazardous practices that may result in severe injury, death, and/or property damage.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{NOTE}: Hazardous practices that may result in equipment damage, data loss, performance deterioration, or unanticipated results.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{TIP}: Supplements the important information or operation tips in the text.

\end{itemize}


\subsection{Symbols}
\label{\detokenize{index:symbols}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Warning and Caution symbols: Must read to alert individuals to potential hazards or risks.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Read the user manual before operation.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Do not dismantle the product.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Keep the product away from fire.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Keep away from children.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
This symbol indicates that a lithium\sphinxhyphen{}ion (Li\sphinxhyphen{}ion) battery is inside the product and should be disposed of or recycled properly.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
This symbol indicates that the product shall not be disposed of as household waste, and should be delivered to a designated collection facility for recycling.
Proper disposal and recycling can help protect the environment. For more information about the disposal and recycling of this product, contact your local community, disposal service, or dealer.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Risk of electric shock.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Battery charging.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Explosive material.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Heavy object.

\end{itemize}

\section{What’s in the Box}
\label{\detokenize{index:what-s-in-the-box}}

\subsection{Included Items}
\label{\detokenize{index:included-items}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Jackery HomePower 2000 Plus v2

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
AC charging cable

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
User manual

\end{itemize}

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{tip}{Tip:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The car charging cable is not included but is available for purchase separately on the website.
For assistance, please contact customer service.
\end{sphinxadmonition}


\subsection{FCC Compliance (Part 15)}

This equipment has been tested and found to comply with the limits for a Class B digital device,
pursuant to Part 15 of the FCC Rules.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This device complies with Part 15 of the FCC Rules. Operation is subject to the following two conditions:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{(}{)}%

\sphinxAtStartPar
This device may not cause harmful interference, and

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
This device must accept any interference received, including interference that may cause undesired operation.

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxAtStartPar
These limits are designed to provide reasonable protection against harmful interference in a residential installation.
This equipment generates, uses and can radiate radio frequency
\end{sphinxadmonition}

\HBApplyLang{en}

\section{LCD Display}
\label{\detokenize{index:lcd-display}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The LCD display shows the current working status of the Jackery HomePower 2000 Plus v2.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Displayed information typically includes:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Battery level

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Input power

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Output power

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Charging status

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Error indicators

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
UPS mode indicator

\end{itemize}

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{note}{Note:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The detailed LCD icon explanations are presented with a numbered diagram
in the official manual.
\end{sphinxadmonition}

\section{Operating Instructions}
\label{\detokenize{index:operating-instructions}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This section describes the basic operation procedures of the Jackery HomePower 2000 Plus v2.


\subsection{Power On / Off}
\label{\detokenize{index:power-on-off}}\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Press the power button to turn on the unit.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The LCD screen will light up and display system status.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Press the power button again to turn off the system.

\end{enumerate}


\subsection{AC Output}
\label{\detokenize{index:ac-output}}\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Press the AC button to enable AC output.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The AC indicator will appear on the display.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Connect your device to the AC output ports.

\end{enumerate}


\subsection{USB Output}
\label{\detokenize{index:usb-output}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
USB ports provide direct DC output for compatible devices such as smartphones,
tablets and cameras.


\subsection{DC Output}
\label{\detokenize{index:dc-output}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The DC output ports can be used for powering compatible 12V devices.

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{note}{Note:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Detailed interface diagrams and operating illustrations are provided in
the visual pages of the final manual.
\end{sphinxadmonition}

\HBApplyLang{en}


\section{UPS Mode}
\label{\detokenize{index:ups-mode}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Jackery HomePower 2000 Plus v2 supports Uninterruptible Power Supply (UPS) functionality.

\sphinxAtStartPar
When the unit is connected to an AC power source, it can supply power to
connected devices while simultaneously charging its internal battery.

\sphinxAtStartPar
In the event of a power outage:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The system automatically switches to battery power.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Connected devices continue to receive power without interruption.

\end{itemize}


\subsection{Typical Use Cases}
\label{\detokenize{index:typical-use-cases}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
UPS mode is suitable for powering devices that require continuous power supply, such as:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Desktop computers

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Network routers

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Security systems

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Home office equipment

\end{itemize}

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{caution}{Caution:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Ensure the total load does not exceed the rated output power of the unit.
\end{sphinxadmonition}

\HBApplyLang{en}


\section{Battery Pack Expansion}
\label{\detokenize{index:battery-pack-expansion}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Jackery HomePower 2000 Plus v2 supports expansion with compatible external battery packs.


\subsection{Connecting Battery Packs}
\label{\detokenize{index:connecting-battery-packs}}\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Ensure the power station is turned off.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Connect the battery expansion cable to the expansion port.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Secure the connection properly.

\end{enumerate}


\subsection{Benefits}
\label{\detokenize{index:benefits}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Adding expansion battery packs can provide:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Increased energy storage capacity

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Extended operating time

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Improved flexibility for different power demands

\end{itemize}

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{note}{Note:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Refer to the compatible battery pack models for supported configurations.
\end{sphinxadmonition}

\HBApplyLang{en}


\section{Charging the Power Station}
\label{\detokenize{index:charging-the-power-station}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Jackery HomePower 2000 Plus v2 supports multiple charging methods.


\subsection{AC Charging}
\label{\detokenize{index:ac-charging}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Connect the AC charging cable to a wall outlet and plug the other end into the
AC input port of the power station.


\subsection{Solar Charging}
\label{\detokenize{index:solar-charging}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The power station can be charged using compatible solar panels.

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{note}{Note:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Ensure the solar panel voltage and current are within the supported range.
\end{sphinxadmonition}


\subsection{Car Charging}
\label{\detokenize{index:car-charging}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The power station can also be charged through a vehicle’s 12V car outlet.

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{tip}{Tip:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Car charging may take longer compared with AC charging.
\end{sphinxadmonition}

\HBApplyLang{en}


\section{Storage and Maintenance}
\label{\detokenize{index:storage-and-maintenance}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Proper storage and maintenance can help prolong the lifespan of Jackery HomePower 2000 Plus v2.


\subsection{Storage Guidelines}
\label{\detokenize{index:storage-guidelines}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Store the product in a dry and cool environment.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Avoid exposure to direct sunlight.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Keep the device away from water and high humidity.

\end{itemize}


\subsection{Battery Maintenance}
\label{\detokenize{index:battery-maintenance}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
If the power station will not be used for an extended period:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Charge the battery to approximately 60–80\%.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Recharge the battery every 3–6 months.

\end{itemize}

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{caution}{Caution:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Failure to maintain battery charge may result in battery degradation.
\end{sphinxadmonition}

\HBApplyLang{en}


\section{Troubleshooting}
\label{\detokenize{index:troubleshooting}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
If the power station does not operate as expected, refer to the following guidelines.


\subsection{Device Not Powering On}
\label{\detokenize{index:device-not-powering-on}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Possible causes:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Battery is completely discharged

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Power button not pressed properly

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Solution:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Recharge the device

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Press the power button again

\end{itemize}


\subsection{No Output Power}
\label{\detokenize{index:no-output-power}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Possible causes:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Output port disabled

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Overload protection activated

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Solution:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Check the output settings

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Remove excessive loads

\end{itemize}


\subsection{Charging Issues}
\label{\detokenize{index:charging-issues}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Possible causes:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Incorrect charging cable

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Power source unavailable

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Solution:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Verify cable connections

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Check power supply source

\end{itemize}

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{note}{Note:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
If the issue persists, contact customer support.
\end{sphinxadmonition}

\HBApplyLang{en}


\section{Warranty}
\label{\detokenize{index:warranty}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Jackery HomePower 2000 Plus v2 is covered by a limited warranty provided by the manufacturer.


\subsection{Warranty Coverage}
\label{\detokenize{index:warranty-coverage}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The warranty covers defects in materials and workmanship under normal use.


\subsection{Warranty Period}
\label{\detokenize{index:warranty-period}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The warranty period begins on the date of purchase.


\subsection{Exclusions}
\label{\detokenize{index:exclusions}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The warranty does not cover:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Damage caused by misuse

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Unauthorized modifications

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Accidental damage

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Improper storage conditions

\end{itemize}


\subsection{Customer Support}
\label{\detokenize{index:customer-support}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
For warranty service or technical support, contact the official support channel.

\HBApplyLang{en}


\section{App Setup}
\label{\detokenize{index:app-setup}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Jackery HomePower 2000 Plus v2 can be monitored and controlled using the official mobile application.


\subsection{Basic Features}
\label{\detokenize{index:basic-features}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Real\sphinxhyphen{}time power monitoring

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Input and output status display

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Battery level monitoring

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Firmware updates

\end{itemize}

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{note}{Note:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The official manual provides detailed step\sphinxhyphen{}by\sphinxhyphen{}step screenshots
for connecting the device to the mobile application.
\end{sphinxadmonition}

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{note}{Note:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
TODO: Insert app connection screenshots and pairing instructions.
\end{sphinxadmonition}
